title: Multiple sclerosis — strong, contradicted, implied
notes: Synthetic; controlled ground truth. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: The therapy reduced the annualised relapse rate compared with the active comparator.1 The therapy halted disability progression.1 The therapy reduced the number of new MRI lesions.2

goldClaims.originalText: The therapy reduced the annualised relapse rate compared with the active comparator.1
goldClaims.text: The therapy reduced the annualised relapse rate compared with the active comparator

goldClaims.originalText: The therapy reduced the number of new MRI lesions.2
goldClaims.text: The therapy reduced the number of new MRI lesions

1.name: MS-COMPARE (Fischer 2024)
1.text: The annualised relapse rate was significantly lower with the therapy than the active comparator (rate ratio 0.62; p<0.001). Confirmed disability progression did not differ significantly between the two groups.

2.name: MS-COMPARE MRI
2.text: Table 3. New or enlarging T2 lesions over 96 weeks: therapy 1.2, comparator 3.8 (mean per patient).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The therapy reduced the annualised relapse rate compared with the active comparator
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref states the relapse-rate reduction (rate ratio 0.62, p<0.001) explicitly.
Ref says confirmed disability progression did not differ between groups — negates 'halted' (rule 1).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The therapy reduced the number of new MRI lesions
goldVerdicts.rationale: Support is a lesion-count data table (1.2 vs 3.8) with no narrative sentence (rule 5).
